cases.prompt: Build the trace widget for terminal sessions so I can watch what lanes are doing and quickly tell if one is drifting.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Trace widget
Object: terminal trace widget for lane monitoring.
Goal: let operators tell quickly when a lane is drifting.
Constraints: stay terminal-first and low friction.
Next: sketch the event inputs and compact panel layout.

generic_checklist.summary: Build the terminal trace widget for lane monitoring.
generic_checklist.scope: Terminal-first lane visibility in active ORP sessions.
generic_checklist.approach: Start with the event inputs and a compact summary row.
checks: An operator can identify a drifting lane quickly.
risks: Too much noise if every event is surfaced.
generic_checklist.handoff: Define the event schema and first rendering pass.
generic_checklist.notes: Use the trace widget as a first debugging surface.

kernel_artifact.goal: surface lane drift and state clearly for operators
success_criteria: an operator can identify a drifting lane within 10 seconds
the widget does not overload the terminal surface

cases.prompt: Decide whether the web app home should lead with linked projects or the old idea board.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Home surface direction
Question: should the web app lead with linked projects or the old idea board?
Decision: lead with linked projects first.
Why: active work should be foregrounded and idea browsing can move into Pensieve.
Tradeoffs: the old idea board becomes secondary navigation.

generic_checklist.summary: Choose the linked-project home instead of the old idea-board-first home.
constraints: preserve access to the full idea library
generic_checklist.approach: Lead with linked projects and move broader idea browsing into Pensieve.
checks: Users can reach active work in one click.
risks: Idea discovery becomes less prominent on the home screen.
generic_checklist.handoff: Implement the linked-project home shell and lower-right Pensieve access.
generic_checklist.notes: Alternative considered: keep the idea board as the front door.

kernel_artifact.question: Should the web app home foreground linked projects or the old idea board?
kernel_artifact.chosen_path: Foreground linked projects and move broad idea browsing into Pensieve.
rejected_alternatives: keep the old idea board as the default home
split the home evenly between ideas and projects
kernel_artifact.rationale: Active work should be reachable immediately, while the idea library can stay available as secondary navigation.
consequences: linked projects become the primary home object
idea browsing becomes one click deeper

cases.prompt: Hypothesize whether drift summaries will help operators notice stalled lanes faster.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Drift summary hypothesis
Claim: short drift summaries will help operators notice stalled lanes faster.
Scope: terminal-first multi-lane work.
Assumptions: operators glance at summaries while they work.
Test: compare stalled-lane pickup with and without summaries.

generic_checklist.summary: Drift summaries will improve stalled-lane pickup speed.
constraints: operators cannot be flooded with noise
generic_checklist.approach: Add summaries and compare pickup behavior against a no-summary baseline.
risks: Summaries may be ignored if they are too verbose.
generic_checklist.handoff: Design the comparison fixture and timing capture.
generic_checklist.notes: Assumes operators actually consult the summary lane.

kernel_artifact.claim: Short drift summaries reduce the time needed to identify stalled lanes.
assumptions: operators glance at summaries while they work
summaries do not introduce excessive noise
kernel_artifact.test_path: Run matched stalled-lane pickup trials with and without summaries and compare detection time.
falsifiers: pickup time does not improve materially

cases.prompt: Design an experiment to compare kernel tasks versus free-form tasks for handoff pickup.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Kernel handoff experiment
Objective: compare free-form tasks, checklist tasks, and kernel tasks during handoff pickup.
Method: give matched task artifacts to a second operator and time correct interpretation.
Evidence: collect scores and clarification counts.
Limits: internal sample only.

generic_checklist.summary: Compare handoff pickup across free-form, checklist, and kernel task artifacts.
generic_checklist.approach: Give a second operator one artifact at a time and record time to correct interpretation.
generic_checklist.handoff: Prepare the prompt set and reviewer score sheet.
generic_checklist.notes: This is a first comparative signal, not a final outcome study.

kernel_artifact.objective: Measure whether kernel task artifacts improve handoff pickup quality over free-form and generic checklist alternatives.
kernel_artifact.method: Run matched handoff trials where a second operator receives one artifact at a time and explains the task, constraints, and next action.
interpretation_limits: the pilot measures structural pickup, not full downstream outcomes

cases.prompt: Record the current state after stabilizing runner routing for Habanero.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Habanero routing checkpoint
Completed: restored linked-project routing for Habanero.
Current state: the repo is bound and the primary session is routable again.
Risks: other machines may still need a sync.
Next: rerun runner sync on active machines.

generic_checklist.summary: Restored the Habanero linked-project routing path.
generic_checklist.scope: Habanero runner routing and session availability.
generic_checklist.approach: Normalize the local link state and resync runner state.
checks: The linked project is routable again.
risks: Other active machines may still carry stale routing state.
generic_checklist.handoff: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify live pickup.
generic_checklist.notes: The local repo, primary session, and hosted world are aligned again.

kernel_artifact.completed_unit: Restored canonical runner routing for Habanero.
kernel_artifact.current_state: The local project link, primary session, and hosted world are synchronized and routable again.
risks: inactive machines may still hold stale routing state
older queued jobs may need a fresh sync before pickup
kernel_artifact.next_handoff_target: Rerun runner sync on active machines and verify one fresh hosted job pickup.

cases.prompt: Define the routing policy for hosted runner jobs so they only target routeable linked sessions.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Runner routing policy
Rule: only claim hosted jobs for linked projects that have a routeable local session.
Scope: hosted runner pickup.
Why: avoid claiming work with nowhere real to execute.
Invariants: a claimed job must resolve to an actual local session.

generic_checklist.summary: Only claim hosted jobs for linked projects that have a routeable local session.
generic_checklist.approach: Require synced linked-project state and a live local session before claiming work.
risks: Stale sync state can hide a routeable session.
generic_checklist.handoff: Audit pickup behavior against stale-session and missing-session cases.
generic_checklist.notes: The rule exists to prevent dead-end job claims.

kernel_artifact.scope: Hosted runner job pickup and claim behavior.
kernel_artifact.rule: Only claim a hosted job when the linked project has a routeable local session on the current machine.
kernel_artifact.rationale: Jobs should only be claimed when the runner can execute them against a real local target.
invariants: a claimed job must resolve to an actual local session
kernel_artifact.enforcement_surface: runner sync, poll, and work lifecycle

cases.prompt: Summarize the result of shipping the first reasoning kernel release and what still needs follow-up.
cases.freeform_markdown:
# Kernel launch result
Claim: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release.
Evidence: validation docs, benchmarks, and shipped CLI commands.
Status: shipped in the CLI.
Next: run comparative studies against free-form artifacts and checklist artifacts.

generic_checklist.summary: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release in the CLI.
generic_checklist.scope: Kernel schema, scaffold, validate, and gate support.
generic_checklist.approach: Document the shipped surface and the next validation studies.
risks: The current evidence is still stronger for implementation validity than outcome superiority.
generic_checklist.handoff: Run comparative artifact and handoff studies next.
generic_checklist.notes: The release is solid as a protocol surface but not yet a proven superior methodology.

kernel_artifact.claim: ORP shipped the first reasoning kernel release as a real CLI protocol surface.
kernel_artifact.status: shipped in the ORP CLI and published to npm
interpretation_limits: comparative superiority over free-form and checklist alternatives is not yet proven
the current evidence is strongest on internal validity
kernel_artifact.next_follow_up: Run the comparative artifact, pickup, and corpus-fit studies.
